title: The Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
description: Introduces abstraction, programming, recursion, higher-order functions, and interpreters in the Berkeley introductory CS sequence.
notes: The catalog page notes prior programming experience equivalent to AP Computer Science A or concurrent math preparation may be helpful, but this curated assistant does not automatically evaluate those non-course conditions.

title: Data Structures
description: Continues the Berkeley CS sequence with linked data structures, trees, graphs, asymptotic analysis, and software engineering practice.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61A, COMPSCI 88, or ENGIN 7 as preparation.

title: Data Structures and Programming Methodology
description: Lab-intensive alternative to COMPSCI 61B covering data structures, abstraction, testing, and implementation practice.
notes: The catalog treats COMPSCI 61BL as an alternative pathway that can satisfy downstream prerequisites where 61B is accepted.

title: Machine Structures
description: Covers machine structures, C programming, memory, synchronization, and the hardware-software interface.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61A and either COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL.

title: Discrete Mathematics and Probability Theory
description: Introduces logic, proof techniques, combinatorics, probability, modular arithmetic, and graph-theoretic tools used across upper-division CS.
notes: The catalog emphasizes mathematical maturity; this assistant does not enforce non-course readiness language automatically.

title: Multivariable Calculus
description: Introduces partial derivatives, multiple integrals, vector calculus, and geometric applications commonly used in advanced computing and machine learning.
notes: The catalog page assumes prior calculus preparation; this assistant treats MATH 53 as a standalone requirement when it appears in course prerequisites.

title: Linear Algebra and Differential Equations
description: Covers matrices, vector spaces, eigenvalues, systems of differential equations, and modeling tools used in many upper-division CS courses.
notes: The Berkeley CS BA page lists MATH 54 as part of lower-division preparation options.

title: Computer Architecture and Engineering
description: Upper-division hardware and architecture course focused on processor, memory, and performance design.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61C as preparation.

title: User Interface Design and Development
description: Project-based interface design course covering prototyping, user research, and interactive system implementation.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL as prerequisites.

title: Computer Security
description: Security-focused upper-division course covering vulnerabilities, defenses, cryptography applications, and secure systems thinking.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61B, COMPSCI 61C, and COMPSCI 70.

title: Operating Systems and System Programming
description: Systems course covering operating systems, concurrency, virtualization, synchronization, and distributed-system fundamentals.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61B, COMPSCI 61C, and COMPSCI 70.

title: Programming Languages and Compilers
description: Explores interpreters, type systems, compiler construction, language design, and runtime implementation.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61B and COMPSCI 61C.

title: Introduction to the Internet: Architecture and Protocols
description: Networking course covering Internet architecture, protocols, routing, reliability, and network applications.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61B; COMPSCI 61C is recommended background rather than a formal prerequisite.

title: Introduction to Software Engineering
prerequisites: COMPSCI C88C OR DATA C88C OR COMPSCI 61A OR COMPSCI 47A
description: Software engineering course centered on agile methods, testing, service deployment, and team-based application development.
notes: The catalog lists one introductory programming prerequisite and one data structures prerequisite; multiple Berkeley alternatives are accepted.

title: Efficient Algorithms and Intractable Problems
description: Core algorithms course covering greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, graph algorithms, NP-completeness, and reductions.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61B and COMPSCI 70.

title: Computational Biology
description: Applies algorithmic and probabilistic methods to molecular biology, sequence analysis, and biological data.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 70 and COMPSCI 170.

title: Introduction to Database Systems
description: Introduces database design, SQL, storage, query processing, transactions, and data systems implementation.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61C or COMPSCI 47C; it also notes COMPSCI 47C may be taken as a co-requisite for transfer students.
co_requisite_policy: COMPSCI 47C may be taken concurrently only for transfer students; otherwise one of the listed prerequisites should already be complete.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
description: AI survey course covering search, probabilistic reasoning, Markov decision processes, and machine learning foundations.
notes: The catalog lists COMPSCI 61A, COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL, and COMPSCI 70.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Machine learning course covering linear models, optimization, probabilistic inference, and supervised and unsupervised learning.
notes: The catalog lists MATH 53, MATH 54, and COMPSCI 70, with instructor consent noted as an alternate path that this assistant does not auto-approve.
consent_exception: The catalog notes instructor consent as an alternate path to the listed prerequisites.

title: Social Implications of Computer Technology
description: Examines ethical, policy, labor, privacy, and societal impacts of computing technologies.
notes: This course has no structured prerequisite chain in the curated records.

title: Principles and Techniques of Data Science
prerequisites: COMPSCI 61A OR COMPSCI C8 OR DATA C8
co_requisites: COMPSCI 61B OR COMPSCI 61BL OR DATA C88C
description: Introduces inferential thinking, computational and statistical foundations, and practical data-science workflows in Berkeley's computing curriculum.
notes: The Berkeley catalog states that students should have completed COMPSCI 61A or COMPSCI C8 or DATA C8 with a grade of C- or better and MATH 54 with a grade of C- or better. It also says students who are concurrently enrolled in COMPSCI 61B, COMPSCI 61BL, or DATA C88C may enroll with instructor permission.
co_requisite_policy: Concurrent enrollment in COMPSCI 61B, COMPSCI 61BL, or DATA C88C is permitted only with instructor permission.
consent_exception: The catalog allows instructor permission for the concurrent-enrollment pathway.
aliases: Principles and Techniques of Data Science

title: Introduction to Modern Biostatistical Theory and Practice
description: Applied statistics and data-analysis course connecting computation, modeling, inference, and modern biostatistical practice.
notes: The Berkeley catalog states that COMPSCI C100 or COMPSCI 189 must be completed with a grade of C- or better before enrollment.
